# Tidal Archive ribbon photographs

This directory contains five original AI-generated photographs created specifically for the `bending-webgl-gallery-ribbon` demo. Together they form a fictional architectural photo essay: approach a small coastal archive at dawn, cross its cobalt threshold, inspect the circular light well and material junction, then return outside at blue hour.

## AI disclosure and use

- Generated on 2026-07-21 (Asia/Shanghai) with Codex's built-in `image_gen` tool.
- All architecture, locations and events shown are fictional. These images must not be described as documentary photographs of a real building or coast.
- Image 1 was generated from text. Images 2–5 used the preceding generated outputs as visual references to preserve the pavilion, material and film identity.
- No real person, brand, logo or source photograph was requested or supplied.
- The selected built-in outputs were 1122 × 1402 PNG files. Project copies were resized to 720 × 900 RGB JPEG with macOS `sips`, JPEG quality 88. The original generated PNGs remain in the built-in generated-image store.
- The images are core WebGL media-plane inputs. They are decoded into p5 textures and rendered on a cylindrically bent ribbon; they are not decorative HTML backgrounds.

## Complete prompts

### 01 · Dawn approach

```text
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: first frame in a WebGL design and photography archive film ribbon
Primary request: Create the establishing photograph for a fictional architectural photo essay titled only in metadata, never inside the image: a small monolithic cast-concrete tidal archive pavilion on a remote black basalt coast.
Scene/backdrop: dawn after rain, low winter sea, wet basalt shelves, pale mist, the pavilion slightly elevated above tide pools.
Subject: the same distinctive pavilion that later frames can revisit: rough pale-gray board-formed concrete, one narrow cobalt-blue entry portal, a recessed warm amber interior, a circular roof light well, no people.
Style/medium: believable high-end editorial architecture photography shot on medium-format color negative film; natural film grain, restrained dynamic range, precise material texture, not a render.
Composition/framing: portrait 4:5 frame, three-quarter approach view, pavilion centered slightly high, a clear foreground path of wet basalt leading to the blue portal, bold silhouette readable at thumbnail scale.
Lighting/mood: blue dawn, diffused overcast sky, subtle warm interior counterpoint, quiet observational mood.
Color palette: basalt black, fog gray, concrete bone, cobalt blue, amber.
Constraints: entirely fictional architecture; no signage, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no people, no vehicles, no watermark; coherent physically plausible construction; edge-to-edge photograph.
Avoid: impossible cantilevers, warped walls, fantasy sci-fi forms, hyper-saturated HDR, excessive fog, duplicate doors, pseudo-text, borders or graphic layout.
```

### 02 · Cobalt threshold

```text
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: second frame in a continuous WebGL architectural photo archive
Input image: use the immediately preceding generated pavilion photograph as the reference image for exact architecture, materials, cobalt portal, circular roof opening, coastal weather, film stock and color language. This is a new photograph in the same fictional essay, not an edit displayed side by side.
Primary request: Photograph the approach at the threshold of the exact same tidal archive pavilion several minutes later.
Scene/backdrop: the camera is much closer and slightly to the left of the cobalt-blue portal; wet board-formed concrete fills most of the frame, with a narrow glimpse of the black coast and pale sea behind.
Subject: the same distinctive cobalt entry plane, warm amber inner wall, concrete grain and salt-water traces; no people.
Style/medium: believable high-end editorial architecture photography on the same medium-format color negative film, natural grain and subdued contrast.
Composition/framing: portrait 4:5, oblique close architectural view; blue portal forms one strong vertical third, amber passage recedes inward, enough context to recognize the same building.
Lighting/mood: cool blue dawn with slightly brighter ambient light, warm passage glow, quiet and tactile.
Color palette: concrete gray, cobalt blue, wet basalt, restrained amber.
Constraints: preserve the building identity and physically plausible geometry from the reference; no signage, text, numbers, logos, people, vehicles, watermark, borders or graphic layout.
Avoid: inventing extra doors/windows, fisheye distortion, fantasy forms, hyper-saturated HDR, pseudo-text.
```

### 03 · Circular light well

```text
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: third frame in a continuous WebGL architectural photo archive
Input images: use the two immediately preceding generated photographs as references for the exact same fictional tidal pavilion, cobalt portal, rough board-formed concrete, amber warmth, coastal dawn and medium-format film language.
Primary request: Create the first interior photograph after passing through that portal, looking upward and across the pavilion's compact central room.
Scene/backdrop: an austere concrete interior open to the pale morning through the same circular roof light; a shallow rain-darkened stone floor, one low built-in travertine bench, a narrow cobalt-blue edge of the entrance visible behind, sea haze faintly visible through the doorway.
Subject: the circular light well casting a soft oval pool of cold daylight onto tactile concrete, with a small warm amber wall wash continuing the sequence; no people or objects suggesting habitation.
Style/medium: believable high-end editorial architecture photography on the same medium-format color negative film, natural grain, subdued contrast, real lens perspective.
Composition/framing: portrait 4:5, low eye-level interior view, circular opening and light pool form the main graphic shapes, clear depth and physically plausible room geometry, readable at thumbnail scale.
Lighting/mood: quiet cool morning, natural skylight, restrained amber bounce, meditative but documentary.
Color palette: concrete bone and gray, cobalt accent, pale sky blue, small amber glow.
Constraints: same building identity; no signage, text, numbers, logos, people, furniture beyond the single built-in bench, watermark, borders or graphic layout.
Avoid: impossible openings, cavernous scale, luxury decor, surreal light beams, fantasy sci-fi, HDR, pseudo-text.
```

### 04 · Material junction

```text
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: fourth frame, a material-study photograph in a continuous WebGL architectural archive
Input images: use the three preceding generated photographs as references for the exact fictional tidal pavilion, cobalt entry element, board-formed concrete, wet black stone, amber/cool-blue palette and medium-format film look.
Primary request: Create an intimate material study made inside the same pavilion: the junction where the cobalt-painted steel entry plane meets rain-darkened basalt flooring and rough board-formed concrete, with sea-salt droplets and a narrow reflection of the circular skylight.
Scene/backdrop: close architectural detail at the threshold, physically plausible construction joint, a sliver of pale ocean and black coast softly out of focus beyond.
Subject: tactile surfaces and joinery—cobalt painted steel edge, concrete lift lines, wet basalt, salt crystals, restrained amber reflection; no hands, tools or people.
Style/medium: believable editorial architecture detail photography on the same medium-format color negative film; shallow but not extreme depth of field, natural fine grain, documentary rather than product advertising.
Composition/framing: portrait 4:5, strong diagonal junction and one cobalt vertical, macro-to-medium close view; simple shapes remain legible as a small film frame.
Lighting/mood: cool late-morning coastal light, quiet, precise, tactile.
Color palette: cobalt blue, charcoal basalt, bone-gray concrete, tiny amber reflection.
Constraints: same materials and building identity; no signage, text, numbers, logos, people, watermark, borders, graphic layout or implausible hardware.
Avoid: abstract wallpaper, impossible seams, excessive bokeh, shiny CGI materials, saturated HDR, pseudo-text.
```

### 05 · Blue-hour return

```text
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: fifth and closing frame in a continuous WebGL architectural photo archive
Input images: use the four preceding generated photographs as references for the exact same fictional tidal pavilion, its monolithic proportions, circular roof opening, cobalt portal, board-formed concrete, wet basalt site and medium-format editorial film character.
Primary request: Create the closing exterior photograph at blue hour, returning to the same pavilion from the seaward side after the tide has risen.
Scene/backdrop: black basalt and a shallow tide pool in the foreground, calm dark ocean behind; the compact concrete volume seen from a rear three-quarter angle, circular roof opening still recognizable, cobalt portal visible as a thin edge, warm amber interior reflected in the pool.
Subject: the same pavilion at the end of the photo walk, no new wings or openings, no people.
Style/medium: believable high-end editorial architecture photography on the same medium-format color negative film; restrained grain, deep but detailed shadows, natural optics, not a render.
Composition/framing: portrait 4:5; pavilion occupies the central upper half, tide-pool reflection leads upward, horizon remains level, clear silhouette at thumbnail scale.
Lighting/mood: deep blue hour just after sunset, soft coastal mist, warm amber interior as the only bright accent, conclusive and calm.
Color palette: midnight blue, charcoal basalt, concrete gray, thin cobalt accent, warm amber.
Constraints: preserve building identity and physically plausible site; entirely fictional; no signage, letters, numbers, logos, people, vehicles, watermark, borders or graphic layout.
Avoid: extra windows, fantasy illumination, starry sci-fi sky, extreme long exposure, hyper-saturated teal-orange grading, warped architecture, pseudo-text.
```
